ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device (e.g., 82541xx, 82547GI/EI) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Unique serial number for each device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the device manufacturer */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the device */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the device */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the device was installed */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the device information was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the setting belongs to */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting (e.g., Asymmetric Pause, Remote Fault) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the setting (e.g., 0b, 1b) */,
  "setting_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the setting type */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the setting value (e.g., integer, boolean, string) */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the setting */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum value for the setting (if applicable) */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum value for the setting (if applicable) */,
  "is_required" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the setting is required */,
  "is_read_only" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the setting is read-only */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_settings_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_settings_setting_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("setting_type_id") REFERENCES settings_types ("setting_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the configuration belongs to */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration */,
  "config_data" TEXT /* Configuration data */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the current version of the configuration */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the configuration */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the configuration was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the configuration */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date the configuration was last modified */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the configuration is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration the version belongs to */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the configuration */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the version */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "changelog" TEXT /* Description of changes made in this version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., network administrator, engineer, technician) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date the user last logged in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the device type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device type */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the device manufacturer */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the device type */,
  "supported_protocols" TEXT /* List of supported network protocols */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings_types" (
  "setting_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting type */,
  "setting_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting type */,
  "is_required" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the setting type is required */,
  "is_read_only" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the setting type is read-only */,
  "supported_data_types" TEXT /* List of supported data types for settings of this type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device being accessed */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the device or configuration */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the device or configuration was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., read, write) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the device or configuration */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the device or configuration */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the access was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)
